sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the current positions after the LEFT action.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  green: center={center}, boundary={b}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, so after LEFT:
- Left green: (31, 19) - moved from (31, 22) to (31, 19)
- Right green: (31, 40) - unchanged
So LEFT moved the left green. Now the left green is at (31, 19). From earlier observations, when the left green is at (31, 19), RIGHT moves the right green. Let me verify this by pressing RIGHT and seeing if the right green moves.
But wait, I already tried this earlier and it seemed like RIGHT moved the left green when the left green was at (31, 19). Let me re-check.
Actually, looking at the history:
- After UP, RIGHT: left green moved from (34, 19) to (31, 19) via UP, then from (31, 19) to (31, 22) via RIGHT.
So when the left green was at (31, 19), RIGHT moved the left green to (31, 22). This contradicts my earlier observation that when the left green is at (31, 19), RIGHT moves the right green.
Hmm, maybe I misremembered. Let me just try pressing RIGHT and see what happens.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the control scheme. Maybe the rule is:
- When the left green is at (31, 19) AND the right green is at (31, 40), RIGHT moves the right green.
- When the left green is at (31, 19) AND the right green is NOT at (31, 40), RIGHT moves the left green.
But the right green IS at (31, 40) now. So RIGHT should move the right green.
Actually, I think the control scheme might be:
- When the left green is at (31, 19), LEFT/RIGHT controls the right green.
- When the left green is NOT at (31, 19), LEFT/RIGHT controls the left green.
But earlier, when the left green was at (31, 19), RIGHT moved the left green to (31, 22). So this doesn't match.
Let me just try pressing RIGHT and see what happens. If the right green moves, then the control scheme is as I thought. If the left green moves, then the control scheme is different.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Left green at (31, 19), right green at (31, 40)
# Try RIGHT to see if it moves the right green
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
